selectedQuotes.text: Strong results from how Inquiry / Transaction History improved portfolio visibility for finance oversight. This aligns tightly with how our teams operate.
Excellent progress in how Payments / Billing / Payoff improved portfolio visibility for syndication teams. This gives leadership a stronger line of sight.
Really impressed by how Covenants / Compliance made ownership clearer for analytics partners. This simplifies complex work without losing control.

results.p0ItemTitle: Configurable alert thresholds across governance and servicing workflows

aiEstimate.rationale: Cross-workflow threshold configuration requires shared settings framework, UI implementation across 3+ screens, and testing. Moderate complexity with clear scope.
aiEstimate.risk: Exceeds remaining budget. Requires scope reduction or timeline extension for overnight delivery.

results.p0ItemTitle: Context-rich handoff summaries for risk review and multi-team coordination

aiEstimate.rationale: Multi-screen handoff summary generation requires data aggregation from workflow states, template design, and integration testing across three feature contexts.
aiEstimate.risk: Workflow Engine data model dependency; time pressure if integration complexity exceeds estimates.

latestSynthesisOutput:
## Overview
This Product Requirements Document outlines the development of enhancements to improve syndication workflows, multi-team coordination, and control operations. The focus is on implementing features that support role-based defaults, approval rationale capture, and configurable alert thresholds. These improvements aim to optimize workflow efficiency and enhance portfolio visibility.

## Problem Statement
Current syndication workflows experience bottlenecks during peak workloads due to a lack of role-based defaults. There is also a need for clearer ownership and communication across teams, and existing control mechanisms could benefit from configurable alert thresholds to maintain governance without increasing onboarding friction. These issues hinder overall workflow efficiency and visibility.

## Scope — Tonight's Build
- Implement role-based defaults for syndication workflows.
- Add approval rationale capture functionality for multi-team coordination.
- Introduce configurable alert thresholds for control operations.

## Out of Scope
- Any screens with fewer than 4 submissions.
- Detailed usability review of the Reporting (analytics-inquiry) screen as it requires a separate in-depth analysis.

## User Stories
1. As a syndication manager, I want role-based workflow defaults so that I can handle peak workloads more efficiently.
2. As a team lead, I want to capture approval rationales, so that I can ensure clear ownership of tasks across different teams.
3. As an operations manager, I want configurable alert thresholds, so that I can maintain tight control without increasing onboarding complexity.
4. As an executive, I want improved portfolio visibility, so that I can have a better oversight of financial operations.
5. As a product manager, I want clearer ownership in analytics, so that I can simplify complex tasks without sacrificing control.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Support role-based defaults for smoother syndication workflows:** 
  - Ability to define and apply role-based defaults within the syndication workflow.
  - Successful application of defaults should reduce processing time during peak workloads.
  
## Design Guidance
- Ensure the user interface for role-based defaults is intuitive and integrates seamlessly with existing systems.
- Approval rationale capture should be easy to use and accessible to all relevant team members.
- Configurable alert thresholds should be visibly adjustable and provide clear feedback on current settings.

## Success Metrics
- Reduction in processing time during peak workloads by 20% as a result of role-based defaults.
- Increase in cross-team task completion rate by 15% due to approval rationale capture.
- User satisfaction with control operations measured by a 30% decrease in onboarding-related queries.

macrosActive: Excluded screens with fewer than 4 submissions as per macro instructions.

savedNarrative.opening: Good morning, and welcome back to Emerald 2026. It is 9:00 AM on Day 2 here in Orlando, and we want to start by saying thank you — 2,455 of you took the time yesterday to tell us exactly what you need. We heard you, we got to work, and we are here to show you what happened overnight. We're so excited!
savedNarrative.what_we_heard: Across your inputs, three signals rose to the top. You told us that alert thresholds need to be configurable — not fixed — so teams can govern their own workflows in servicing and beyond. You told us that handoffs between teams break down when context is missing, especially in risk review and multi-team coordination. And your highest-scored request, with 15 votes and a top impact rating, was clear: role-based defaults for syndication and covenant workflows need to exist out of the box.
savedNarrative.what_we_built: Overnight, our team took on the configurable alert thresholds request — the signal that appeared independently in both governance and servicing workflows. What you are about to see is a working prototype built in a single night. It is not a finished product, and it carries no production commitment, but it is real, it runs, and it was shaped entirely by what you told us yesterday.
savedNarrative.what_we_deferred: We want to be straight with you about what we did not build. The context-rich handoff summaries for risk review and multi-team coordination are a medium-complexity effort — too large to do responsibly in one night. We are not setting it aside; we are carrying it forward as a prioritized input, and the signal strength behind it means it will be a serious part of our planning conversations.
savedNarrative.closing: This is what this process is for — not to promise a roadmap on stage, but to show you that your input drives real work. Thank you for the quality and depth of what you shared. Let us walk you through what we built.
